editor.section_order_desc: Drag sections to reorder them on the overview page. Empty sections are hidden automatically. Keyboard users: use the ↑ / ↓ buttons on each row.
editor.weather_sensors_desc: Inline icon+value row rendered at the top of the weather section. Useful for displaying local outdoor sensors (temperature, humidity, wind, pressure).
editor.no_weather_sensors: No sensors configured. Use the picker below to add one.
editor.hide_section_headings_desc: Hides individual section headings on the overview. Useful for compact mobile layouts where the headings add visual noise.
editor.power_badge_entity_desc: Optional. Adds a small live power badge to the overview header (next to person chips) showing the current value of the selected sensor. Pick the entity that represents your current consumption (e.g. grid power, plug power). Requires a sensor with device_class "power" or unit W/kW; otherwise this picker shows no options.
editor.show_unavailable_alert_badge_desc: Adds a small red badge to the overview header showing the count of entities whose state is "unavailable" (broken integration, dead battery, offline device). Auto-hidden when the count is zero. Respects the no_dboard label and per-entity hidden flags.
editor.show_now_playing_badge_desc: Adds a small green badge to the overview header showing the first media_player whose state is "playing". Click opens the media controls. Auto-hidden when nothing is playing.
editor.show_sun_badge_desc: Adds a small badge to the overview header that shows whether the sun is up, and links to the next sunrise/sunset time on tap. Auto-hidden if your HA has no sun.sun entity (the built-in sun integration is enabled by default in most installs).
editor.section_visibility_desc: Optionally hide a section unless a chosen entity is in a specific state. Useful e.g. to show the Agenda only on workdays (entity: calendar.workday_sensor, state: on) or the Energy section only during day hours (entity: sun.sun, state: above_horizon). Leave entity blank to disable per-section.
editor.show_updates_badge_desc: Adds a small orange badge to the overview header showing the count of update.* entities in state "on" (pending). Tap navigates to Settings → Updates. Auto-hidden when no updates are pending. Required integration: none — the update.* domain is HA-native.
editor.show_clock_card_desc: Shows the clock card on the overview.
editor.person_badge_layout_with_state: Picture + name + state (home / away / zone name)
editor.person_badge_layout_desc: What info is shown on each person badge in the header. HA's person entity state automatically becomes the zone name (e.g. "Work") when the person enters a non-home zone, so the with-state layouts double as zone-aware presence. Requires HA zone definitions for multi-zone awareness (built-in).
editor.alarm_desc: Select an alarm control panel entity. When the clock card is active, the alarm panel is shown next to it. Without the clock card, the alarm panel takes the full width.
editor.show_search_card_desc: Shows the custom:search-card directly below the clock on the overview.
editor.show_search_card_missing: Requires custom:search-card and card-tools. Please install both components, or switch the variant below to Native tip (no extra components needed).
editor.show_person_badges: Show person badges in the header
editor.show_person_badges_desc: Auto-generated person chips appear in the overview header by default. Disable when you supply your own person badges via Custom Badges.
editor.search_card_tip_body: Press **Cmd+E** (Mac) or **Ctrl+E** (Win/Linux) anywhere in Home Assistant to open the global quick-bar search. Find entities, run services, open settings — it's the fastest way around HA.
editor.columns_desc: Choose how summary cards should be displayed. The layout adjusts automatically when cards are hidden.
editor.group_lights_by_floors_desc: Groups lights in the lights view by floors. Each floor gets its own section with on/off buttons.
editor.nested_light_groups_desc: Shows light groups as expandable containers with their member lights underneath. Disabled by default to preserve the existing flat list behavior.
editor.lights_sort_by_desc: How lights are ordered inside light groups and room views. "Last changed" is the default and surfaces recently-touched lights first; "Name" gives a stable alphabetical order.
editor.show_partially_open_covers_desc: Shows partially open covers (neither fully open nor closed) in a separate group.
editor.group_covers_by_floors_desc: Groups covers in the covers view by floors. Each floor gets its own subsection with its name as heading. Applies to the main covers group; awnings and windows remain unsplit.
editor.security_extra_entities_desc: Pick any extra entities (smart appliances, custom sensors, etc.) to show as tiles at the bottom of the Security view. Useful for ovens, cooktops, irons — anything you want a glance at before leaving the house.
editor.show_climate_summary_desc: Shows the climate summary card on the overview. Counts active thermostats and climate devices.
editor.hide_mobile_app_batteries_desc: Hides batteries from smartphones, tablets, and watches (Mobile App) from the battery overview and summary.
editor.show_area_in_battery_view: Show area name on battery tiles
editor.show_area_in_battery_view_desc: Prepends the area name to each battery tile in the batteries view (e.g. "Living Room • Window Battery"). Helps distinguish identically named contact sensors that differ only by room.
editor.hide_battery_notes_entities_desc: Hides helper entities created by the HACS Battery Notes integration (battery_type, battery_quantity, battery_low) from the battery view and summary. They're useful elsewhere but aren't real battery levels.
editor.battery_thresholds_desc: Thresholds for battery status groups (Critical / Low / Good).
editor.unavailable_batteries_critical: Show as critical (alerts you to broken sensors)
editor.unavailable_batteries_bucket_desc: Where to bucket battery entities whose state is unavailable, unknown, or non-numeric. "Critical" makes broken sensors visible; "Good" hides them so they don't inflate the critical count.
editor.show_humidity_summary_desc: Adds a Humidity overview view grouping humidity sensors into Dry / Comfortable / Humid buckets. Off by default — enable it if you have humidity sensors. Buckets with no sensors are auto-hidden.
editor.humidity_low_threshold_desc: Humidity readings below this percentage are grouped as "Dry".
editor.humidity_high_threshold_desc: Humidity readings above this percentage are grouped as "Humid"; in between is "Comfortable".
editor.show_weather_desc: Shows the weather forecast card on the overview when a weather entity is available.
editor.show_energy_desc: Shows the energy distribution card on the overview when energy data is available.
editor.light_favorites_desc: Pick lights you want as a compact toggle row on the overview (above the regular favorites). Each light renders as a glance icon: tap to toggle on/off, long-press to open more-info. Auto-hidden when no light is selected. Only light.* entities can be added.
editor.favorites_desc: Select entities to be shown as favorites below the summaries. Entities are displayed as tiles.
editor.group_by_floors_desc: Groups areas on the overview by floors. When enabled, a separate section is created for each floor.
editor.show_switches_on_areas_desc: Shows switches as controls on the area cards in the overview. Home Assistant hides these by default.
editor.show_alerts_on_areas: Show alert icons on area cards
editor.show_alerts_on_areas_desc: Shows alert icons (e.g. motion, moisture, heat) on area cards when a binary sensor in the area is active.
editor.show_window_alerts_on_areas: Show open window/door icons on area cards
editor.show_window_alerts_on_areas_desc: Shows an alert icon on the area card whenever a window, door, opening, or garage-door contact sensor in that area is open. Independent of the regular alert toggle above.
editor.show_locks_in_rooms_desc: Shows locks (e.g. Nuki) in the respective room views. Locks always appear in the security overview regardless.
editor.show_automations_in_rooms_desc: Shows automations assigned to the area in the respective room views.
editor.show_scripts_in_rooms_desc: Shows scripts assigned to the area in the respective room views.
editor.show_cameras_in_rooms_desc: Shows cameras assigned to the area in the respective room views. Disable to keep cameras out of the dashboard without hiding them globally.
editor.hide_unavailable_in_rooms: Hide unavailable entities in room views
editor.hide_unavailable_in_rooms_desc: Skips entities whose state is `unavailable` (offline integrations, dead batteries, disconnected ESPHome devices) when building room views. Default on — these entities show up elsewhere (Batteries view, the optional Unavailable alert badge) and tend to be noise in a room view. Disable to surface them in the room view too.
editor.show_window_contacts_in_rooms: Show window contacts in room views
editor.show_window_contacts_in_rooms_desc: Shows window contacts as badges in the respective room views.
editor.show_door_contacts_in_rooms: Show door contacts in room views
editor.show_door_contacts_in_rooms_desc: Shows door contacts as badges in the respective room views.
editor.use_default_area_sort_desc: Uses the area sorting from Home Assistant instead of the order configured here.
editor.areas_manage_desc: Choose which areas should be shown on the dashboard and in what order. Expand areas to manage individual entities.
editor.room_visibility_desc: Optionally hide a whole room view (and its nav tab) unless a chosen entity is in a specific state. Useful for guest mode rooms (entity: input_boolean.guest_mode, state: on), seasonal rooms (e.g. garden in summer), or rooms that only apply when someone's home. The area cards on the overview are NOT affected — use the area-list above to hide cards there.
editor.room_pins_desc: Select entities to be shown first in their assigned rooms. Ideal for entities that are not automatically detected (e.g. weather stations, special sensors). Only entities with room assignment can be selected. These pins only appear in their respective room, not on the overview.
editor.room_pins_position_desc: Where the Room Pins section appears in each room view. "Top" matches the documentation and the original intent; "Bottom" preserves the legacy ordering for dashboards that relied on it.
editor.show_summary_views_desc: Shows the summary views (Lights, Covers, Security, Batteries) in the top navigation.
editor.show_room_views_desc: Shows individual room views in the top navigation.
editor.custom_cards_desc: Heading and icon for the custom cards section on the dashboard. Leave empty for defaults.
editor.custom_cards_help: Add custom cards to the overview. Cards appear in a separate section between summaries and areas. Tip: Create the card in a regular dashboard first, copy the YAML code, and paste it here.
editor.custom_sections_desc: Declare your own section blocks (each with a heading and a list of Lovelace cards). The section key can be referenced in Section Order and used as a Show in target for custom cards.
editor.custom_sections_help: Power-user escape hatch — add new sections without forking the strategy. Use a unique key, set the heading, paste a YAML list of cards.
editor.custom_badges_help: Add custom badges to the overview header (next to person chips).
editor.custom_views_help: Create custom views with any cards. Tip: Create the view in a regular dashboard first, copy the YAML code, and paste it here.
editor.no_entities_in_area: No entities found in this area
editor.yaml_error_not_object_or_array: YAML must produce an object or an array
editor.yaml_error_not_card_or_list: YAML must produce a card or a list of cards

health.title: Dashboard health check
health.desc: Configuration issues detected. Fixes are one-click for orphaned references; YAML parse errors require editing the relevant tab.
health.orphan_weather_entity_desc: weather_entity points at an entity that is not in your HA registry. Removing it falls back to auto-detection.
health.orphan_power_badge_entity_title: Power badge entity no longer exists
health.orphan_power_badge_entity_desc: power_badge_entity points at an entity that is not in HA. Removing it hides the badge.
health.orphan_house_mode_entity_title: House mode entity no longer exists
health.orphan_house_mode_entity_desc: house_mode_entity points at an entity that is not in HA. Mode-driven section reorder will fall back to the default.
health.orphan_room_pins_desc: Some entities pinned to rooms no longer exist in HA. Removing the stale ones leaves your other pins untouched.
health.orphan_light_favorites_desc: Some entities in your light favorites list no longer exist in HA.
health.orphan_favorite_entities_desc: Some entities in your favorites list no longer exist in HA.
health.orphan_notification_triggers_desc: One or more notification triggers reference entities that no longer exist. These triggers will never fire.
health.orphan_areas_options_desc: areas_options has entries for areas that are not in HA. Safe to drop — the entries have no effect.
health.orphan_custom_views_yaml_desc: One or more custom_views entries have YAML parse errors. Open the Custom Views tab to fix them.
health.orphan_custom_cards_yaml_desc: One or more custom_cards entries have YAML parse errors. Open the Custom Cards tab to fix them.
health.orphan_custom_sections_yaml_desc: One or more custom_sections entries have YAML parse errors. Open the Custom Sections tab to fix them.
health.orphan_custom_badges_yaml_desc: One or more custom_badges entries have YAML parse errors. Open the Custom Badges tab to fix them.
health.muted_camera_companions_desc: You have cameras in HA but the `room_camera_companions` config has some categories disabled. If that was intentional, ignore. Otherwise restore the default to surface all five (spotlight, motion, siren, battery, doorbell).

editor.favorites_split_help: Switch to per-viewport favorites (phone / tablet / wall)
editor.favorites_merge_help: Merges all viewport lists into one, deduplicated. Phone-only and wall-only entries will appear in the unified list.
editor.favorites_flat_hint: Single list shown on every device. Split per device to differentiate.
editor.live_preview_toggle_help: Show the YAML that would be emitted for the current config
editor.room_camera_companions: Companion entities to surface next to each camera (picture-glance):
editor.room_camera_companions_desc: Default: all five enabled. Unchecking all disables picture-glance entirely (cameras fall back to picture-entity).
editor.camera_hero: Camera hero (render the first area camera full-width at the top of the room view)
